Same engines everywhere. CPE is the full hub with built-in levels. New course starts with no folders — only a course name, assignment (edit via the heading menu above), theme (light / dark), and Add a folder when you are ready.

Use of English Level 9 · Part 1, Part 2 & Part 4 (FCE)

Use of English

Part 1 — multiple-choice cloze (A–D). Part 2 — open cloze (one word per gap). Part 4 — key word transformation (B2 First style). From the first Part 1 task you can move through the rest with Next task without returning here. Back in the exercise is one browser step. Submit to check; wrong answers show the key. The collocation sprint (hearts) closes the line after Sounds about right — same phrases from these tasks, embedded so it works offline too.

Lexical games

Definition cards are first: flip to see the phrase, mark what you know. Other modes use up to ten chunks per round; wrong answers often go to the end of the line until the line is clear — then you can earn a checkmark (where shown). On the next screen tick Environment collocations and/or Environment collocations 2 (same engine as Level 9).

Lexical trainer
Open the full typed-gap trainer packs: Environment collocations and Environment collocations 2.
Folder → choose pack
Definition cards
Front: definition / paraphrase — flip: exact phrase from the text. Swipe with Prev / Next; mark I know this or Still learning (saved on this device).
No timer · no checkmark · shuffle each new deck
Lexical drop
A line from the text falls with ____; type the missing chunk. Paraphrase hint below.
Multi-round session · wrong → big answer + next rounds · checkmark when all phrases mastered
Paraphrase pick
English paraphrase on top — tap the exact phrase from the reading (four English options).
Round of 10 · wrong → end of line
60-second express
Speak, then tap: paraphrase or a sentence with ____ — you say the chunk aloud. I knew it adds to your score (on your word). New shuffle every run.
60 s timer · self-check · no checkmark
Echo Run
Listen & transcribe: you hear a chunk from your chosen reading and type it exactly as in the text.
Round of 10 · wrong → end of line
Paraphrase Playback
You hear the paraphrase only (like Treasure Hunt). Type the exact phrase from the reading — nothing from the passage is read aloud.
Round of 10 · wrong → end of line
Pair Blitz
Quizlet-style Match: tap a paraphrase and the phrase from the text. The timer runs until the grid is clear.
Up to 10 pairs · multi-grids · timer

Choose reading

Level 8 · Environment collocations

Tick one or both packs, then Continue. The same Level 9 game screens are used; only items from the ticked packs are in the pool.

Lexical reading trainer

Treasure hunt — Level 8 environment & policy lexis. Match each paraphrase to the exact phrase in the passage. Your total score updates on every correct tap.

A Environment → B Politics & society → C Industry & law

Core phrases: unprecedented rate, breeding ground, shocking levels of pollution, fragile marine ecosystems; prominent campaign group, at an all-time low, appease critics, lack of action on the pledge; dumping hazardous waste, granted more oversight.

Level 8 - Vocabulary (A Environment)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD STYLE)
A Environment

The report documents an unprecedented rate of glacier retreat. Shallow lagoons have turned into a breeding ground for toxic algae, while port cities still register shocking levels of pollution near river mouths. Conservationists warn that fragile marine ecosystems may not bounce back if thresholds are crossed.

Level 8 - Vocabulary (B Politics & society)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD STYLE)
B Politics & society

A prominent campaign group mobilised thousands after public confidence sank at an all-time low. The ministry announced tweaks chiefly to appease critics, yet observers highlighted a serious lack of action on the pledge to end fossil-fuel subsidies.

Level 8 - Vocabulary (C Industry & law)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD STYLE)
C Industry & law

The board was penalised for dumping hazardous waste upstream of the reservoir, and the agency granted more oversight to regional inspectors.

Level 9 · Treasure Hunt (Making a mark)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD STYLE)

Lexical trainer

Reading: The robots are taking over

Use the hint (paraphrase) to recall the chunk, but type only the missing 2–3 words (sometimes one keyword) — the rest of the line is already shown. The counter is only this speaker's items (e.g. 1 / 8 for Jack). Three lives per block; after a wrong check, the answer appears and you must type it to continue.

❤️❤️❤️ 1 / 8
A Jack
Hint (paraphrase)
Take a breath and type what fits — you've prepared for this.

Game over

Out of lives in this speaker's block.

You did it!

Reading: The robots are taking over

You finished every short gap, all five speakers. That takes real stamina — be proud of yourself.

Level 9 - Vocabulary (A Jack)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD STYLE)
A Jack

AI art doesn't concern me in the slightest; however you can understand why it causes so many artists so much discomfort. I suspect it's related to the unpredictability of where it will all end up. For my part, having been involved in a collaborative project recently, I can see how they go hand in hand. I found it fascinating to observe firsthand the interplay of both these creative forms. Artists and AI actually complement each other since one cannot generate output without the other's input. Now that I've had this experience, I'm more convinced. With all that I can bring to the table, I won't be sidelined in the foreseeable future.

Level 9 - Vocabulary (B Valentina)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PARAPHRASE (KEY-WORD READY)
B Valentina

As a graphic designer I know more than most about losing out to AI as our whole industry was turned upside down by new technology in the 80s with many people being made redundant. And this has been replicated more recently in the impact of digital on the music industry as well as print media. People are often reluctant to change, and this is no different, it's just part of the evolution of art. A recent example is graffiti artists who are always being denounced for ruining art when, in actual fact they're a long overdue breath of fresh air in a dusty old artworld. AI is on a collision course with artists for better or worse and that won't be derailed by anything human artists say or do, and nor should it be as far as I'm concerned. So the sooner artists from all walks of life stop deluding themselves that things are going to go just how they'd like them to go the better, because there's little likelihood of that.

Level 9 - Vocabulary (C Phoebe)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PRECISE PARAPHRASE
C Phoebe

You have to hand it to the programmers writing the algorithms and training the AI – the creations are simply incredible by any standard. Would judges in any art competition be able to single out AI generated paintings without exception? To be honest, I wonder whether anyone can reliably answer that as plenty of experts have been fooled by AI generated pieces. For me, it's tantamount to theft, though, when AI reproduces paintings or uses photos without permission. Intellectual property is fundamental to artists to ensure they don't get ripped off and can earn a decent living. Something should be done about this while the genre is still in its infancy, otherwise I imagine we can expect further issues down the line.

Level 9 - Vocabulary (D Richard)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PRECISE PARAPHRASE
D Richard

Many artists seem to fear AI, whereas I'm all for it! In my humble opinion, this is all about artists not wanting to move with the times. I think they are blinkered, in that they don't see the benefits of the technology. Gone are the days when artists could hole themselves up in a remote studio alone, painting in their same old style, and still expect to remain relevant. That kind of lifestyle is out of the question these days and artists can't risk being out of touch with what clients and society want from them, as we're expected to be diverse in what we produce. After all, artists are selling something and those who embrace technology rather than bemoaning the loss of our traditional careers will come across opportunities. They might be surprised by what it's like working with AI, however counterintuitive it may seem.

Level 9 - Vocabulary (E Kiki)

Total score: 0 (+1 each correct match)

PRECISE PARAPHRASE
E Kiki

Nowadays technology steamrolls over everything while genuinely skilled practitioners are left bringing up the rear. If it were up to me, I'd have these AI people spend time with some of our professionals so as to emphasise the hard graft and unique skill that is part and parcel of creativity. You've got to know your Da Vinci from your Kandinsky in terms of objectives, theories and movements as well as have a grasp of differing artistic techniques. It's about representations of the world from different perspectives, not how lifelike the computer image is because it's made from a million pixels.

Sticky notes board
Nine tasks: idioms, prepositions, prep phrases 2, robots, Making a mark, Art & architecture (disk), Art restoration (P3), Contemporary art (Word Bank pack), and Minds eye (Track 10). Unit 10 packs open only from the Unit 10 Word Bank entry or Live on those tasks.

Live room (demo)

Demo + deep link: live_prep=1
Game on!
3:00

Prep vault — escape room

Level 9 · Word Bank packs (pick one or Choose all)

You have 3:00 (timer top right). Use Pack below: one theme or Choose all for a random mix from every Word Bank folder. Each channel draws six random phrases from that pool. Type the missing chunk; Get Hint shows the first letter but costs 20 seconds. I don’t know (button, or type I don’t know and press Check) shows the answer in large capitals, Penalty: −20 sec, then after a short beat this channel gets a new phrase. When a line is correct, a lock channel lights with a key glyph. The vault door opens only when all six are armed. If the clock hits zero, you get System Lockout — use Try Again for a fresh shuffle.

Pack
Secure channel matrix

All six channels match — vault disengaged.

System Lockout

Vault access window closed. The secure channel timed out before all six keys matched.

Create a room, copy the link for the class, and run the round from this bar. Open the app over http(s) with a port, not file://. Scores when a player locks a channel in Prep vault. Admin opens the host dashboard. For Sticky notes, students only see the task you select below.

    Choose reading

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    Tick Reading 7, Contemporary art, Idioms, Listening Part 2 (Making a mark), Listening Part 2 (Painting with the mind’s eye, Track 10), Listening P3 Art restoration, Prepositional phrases, and/or Art & architecture (disk) — same gap / flip / drop mechanics; the trainer uses one pack at a time.

    Lexical drop

    Reading: The robots are taking over

    How it works. You see a full sentence with one gap ____. Only a short chunk (usually about 1–8 words) from the text fills the gap. If Check is wrong or you are too slow, the correct answer appears in large type and that phrase is saved for a later round. Use the paraphrase hint, then type and press Enter or Check before the line hits the bottom.

    Each round you aim for up to ten correct catches; speed is the same for every drop in that round. Rounds 1–4 use the slowest tier, then each new round is a bit faster. Play several rounds until every phrase in this reading has been answered correctly at least once — then you earn a checkmark. Phrases you miss can come back in later rounds.

    Correct wording from the text
    Hint (paraphrase)

    Paraphrase pick

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    You see an English paraphrase (key-word style, like in Treasure Hunt). Below are four English phrases from our texts — tap the one that matches the paraphrase.

    Paraphrase

    Which phrase from the reading matches?

    60-second express

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    How it works. You get 60 seconds. Each card is either a paraphrase (say the exact phrase from the reading) or the same sentence as in Lexical drop with ____ instead of the chunk — say the missing words out loud. When you are satisfied you knew it, press I knew it (that increases your score). Skip goes to the next card without counting. Show answer shows the wording from the text so you can learn; you still choose whether to count the card. The site does not hear you — this is an honour-system drill. Cards are shuffled every run; start again for a new mix from your topic.

    Time left: 60 s I knew it: 0

    From the text

    Time’s up

    You pressed I knew it for 0 card(s). Practise again whenever you like.

    Definition cards

    Level 9 · vocabulary

    How it works. The front shows an English paraphrase (no exact answer, no line from the script, and no Russian — you recall or translate, then flip). The back shows the target phrase and the line from the text; some packs add a short Russian gloss for a quick check. Tap the card (or press Space) to flip. Use Prev / Next to move. Mark I know this / Still learning — saved in the browser per item.

    Definition

    Target phrase

    From the text

    Tap the card to flip · Space when the frame is focused

    Echo Run

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    How it works. Each round has up to ten phrases from your reading. Press Play phrase, type what you heard, then Check. A wrong answer sends that phrase to the end of the line and you hear the next one. When all ten are correct and the line is empty, you get a checkmark. Show answer then Next phrase also sends the item to the end. Uses the browser’s English text-to-speech.

    From the text

    Paraphrase hint

    Paraphrase Playback

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    How it works. Up to ten items per round: you only hear the paraphrase. Type the exact phrase from the reading. A wrong Check sends that item to the end of the line. Show answer then Next phrase does the same. Clear the line with everything correct to earn a checkmark. Uses the browser’s English text-to-speech.

    From the text

    Use the green Live session bar (same on Prep vault / Sticky / Pair Blitz): create room, choose where the link opens, copy for students. Task tabs on Sticky sync when the link uses Sticky notes board.

    Pair Blitz

    Level 9 · The robots are taking over

    How it works. Like Quizlet Match: each screen has up to six paraphrase / text pairs. One round uses up to ten pairs from your reading (fewer if the text is short). After a grid clears, the next set appears until all pairs in the round are matched. The timer runs for the whole round. Wrong taps shake off.

    Time: 0 s Pairs left: 6

    Expand the green Live session bar: create room, pick Students only see (Level 9 tasks 1–9, or Unit 10 Part 4 / books & films only), copy the link. Students open Sticky with that single task — no Prep vault choice on this screen.

    Sticky notes board

    Level 9 · nine sticky tasks

    Unit 10 · two packs (Part 4 · books & films)

    Task 2 — at / in / on / under. Task 3 — compact ____ cues; full lines under Context. Task 1 is idioms only.

    Task 1 — Listening Part 4 (SB 10.1). Task 2 — Describing books & films: same twelve chunks as textbook Exercises 1–2.

    Task 1. One missing word per sticky. Type the missing word and press Check.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 2. Short prepositional chunks: type the missing piece — at, in, on, or under (capitalisation on the note is a hint).

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 3. Prepositional phrases 2 — short skeleton on each sticky (____ = missing piece). The full sentence from the disk Word Bank appears only after you tap Context.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 4. Key lexical chunks from The robots are taking over. One missing word per sticky.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 5. Key chunks from Making a mark. One missing word per sticky.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 6. Key chunks from Different strokes for different folks (interior-design-vocab-core.js). One missing word per sticky.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 7. Key chunks from Listening Part 3 — Art restoration (Track 9.4; art-restoration-data.js). One missing word per sticky.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 8. High-level chunks from the Contemporary art Word Bank pack (u9-must-know-lexis.js). The slot is usually a preposition or particle in the collocation (e.g. with, to, of, at) — not the long content word, so the line reads naturally. Context shows the full sentence from the text.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 9. Full Minds eye Word Bank (Memrise) for Track 10. One missing word per sticky; Context shows the script paragraph.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 10. Listening Part 4 (SB 10.1) phrases only. One missing word per sticky; Context shows the full line.

    Done: 0 / 0

    Task 2. Describing books & films — all twelve chunks from textbook Exercises 1–2 (same word bank as the vocabulary folder).

    Done: 0 / 0
    Speaking trainer
    Step 1 · Read

    A Jack

    Read the whole passage. When you are ready, continue — the full text will hide and only key phrases will stay on screen.

    Step 2 · Retell

    A Jack

    Key phrases only. Press Start retelling when you begin speaking aloud. The ring is your 40-second window (visual mic does not record yet).

    40

    Round finished

    Nice work. Move on when you are ready.

    Speaking trainer
    Freer practice (from reading The robots are taking over)

    Answer the questions

    You get one minute per prompt. Speak out loud as if in an exam or a conversation. The timer starts as soon as a question appears. Use Next question when you are finished before the minute ends, or wait for the ring to complete — then the next prompt loads automatically.

    Each new run shuffles the same set of Level 9-style discussion questions.

    Question 1 / 8

    60

    Answer in full sentences. There is no wrong script — fluency and ideas count.

    Round complete

    You have worked through every prompt in this set. Take a short break, or run the deck again in a new order.

    Speaking trainer
    Idioms: Art and creativity

    Creative process, influence & reactions

    Twelve discussion prompts built around your idiom pack. You get 60 seconds per card. Speak out loud; the timer starts when the question appears. Use Next question to move on early, or wait for the ring — then the next prompt loads.

    Each new run shuffles the order. Themes on screen: Creative process & Results, Influence & Originality, Reactions & Feelings.

    60

    Use the idiom naturally in your answer — fluency and ideas count.

    Round complete

    You have worked through every prompt in this set. Shuffle again or use the top-left back link.

    Speaking trainer
    Micro speaking mode (FCE-style)

    15-second bursts

    Use the target phrase naturally in your own sentence. Unlimited repeats. I used it well adds +1 to your home score and marks a matching item Know in the Word Bank when the wording lines up. Listening = script chunks first, then the nine official sentence-completion keys.
